THOUGHT:
The search returned exactly the target paper: "WILDS: A Benchmark of in-the-Wild Distribution Shifts" by Koh et al. (ICML 2020). This paper is highly relevant to the workshop theme as it directly addresses the performance degradation of ML models when deployed in real-world environments with distribution shifts. It perfectly fits the required citation categories: highlighting research gaps (offline vs. in-the-wild performance), providing a dataset/benchmark reference, comparing empirical findings across domains, and supporting arguments about the causes of deployment failures. I will select this citation and update the description to reflect its specific role in the upcoming manuscript.
